summary: Review completed. The curated Codex shortlist update itself is straightforward, but the current picker still does not apply the displayed default on Enter. That means the init flow continues to persist no explicit Codex model for fresh agents, so the refreshed shortlist does not actually change default runtime selection. This should be treated as a blocker for merge-readiness, not a follow-up.
work_completed: Reviewed the uncommitted diff in `src/control_tower/config_ui.py` and `tests/test_bootstrap.py`.
Inspected `_prompt_backend_and_model` and the agent registry/runtime paths that consume stored model values.

Ran focused verification with `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_bootstrap.BackendTests tests.test_bootstrap.CustomAgentTests.test_interactive_custom_agent_creation tests.test_bootstrap.BootstrapTests.test_interactive_config_quick_mode_keeps_defaults tests.test_bootstrap.BootstrapTests.test_custom_config_can_set_sandboxed_builder`.
Ran an additional interactive init simulation to verify what model values are actually persisted when the user presses Enter through the refreshed Codex picker.

findings.message: `src/control_tower/config_ui.py:442-445` displays the first curated Codex option as the current default (`[gpt-5.4]`) but returns `default_model or ""` when the user presses Enter. For fresh agent setup `default_model` is empty, so accepting the displayed default persists no model at all. I verified this with an interactive init simulation: after choosing `custom` and pressing Enter through all Codex model prompts, the saved registry still had `{'builder': None, 'inspector': None, 'scout': None, 'git-master': None, 'scribe': None}`. Because `runtime_cli.cmd_delegate` uses `model or agent_config.get("model")`, those agents will continue running without `-m gpt-5.4`, so the refreshed shortlist does not actually refresh the init default behavior.

`tests/test_bootstrap.py:2057-2065` locks in the incorrect Enter behavior by asserting that `_prompt_backend_and_model("codex", "")` returns an empty model after showing `gpt-5.4` as the default. The new tests verify ordering and rendering, but they do not cover the intended semantic contract that pressing Enter should select the displayed default and persist it into the saved registry.

follow_up_recommendations: Change `_prompt_backend_and_model` so Enter returns the displayed default model (`models[0]`) when `default_model` is empty, or stop labeling the first option as the default if the product intentionally wants an implicit backend default instead.
Replace the new picker assertion with a behavioral test that verifies Enter on a fresh Codex agent stores `gpt-5.4` and that the saved registry carries that value through init.

Keep using `PYTHONPATH=src` for focused local verification; without it, unittest imports the installed `control_tower` package from `~/.local/share/control-tower/repo/src` instead of the working tree.
memory_worthy: The refreshed Codex picker currently advertises `gpt-5.4` as the default, but Enter still persists no model for fresh agents; merge-readiness is blocked until picker semantics and tests align.
